content.problemStatement:
“You won’t get caught if you hide behind someone.”

Sang-Woo advises Gi-Hun to hide behind someone to avoid getting shot.

Gi-Hun follows Sang-Woo's advice and hides behind Ali, who saved his life earlier. Gi-Hun and Ali both have the same height, KKK. Many players saw this trick and also started hiding behind Ali.

Now, there are NNN players standing between Gi-Hun and Ali in a straight line, with the ithi^{\text{th}}ith player having height HiH_iHi​. Gi-Hun wants to know the minimum number of players who need to get shot so that Ali is visible in his line of sight.

**Note:**


Line of sight is a straight line drawn between the topmost point of two objects. Ali is visible to Gi-Hun if nobody between them crosses this line.
Even if there are some players who have the same height as that of Gi-Hun and Ali, Ali will be visible in Gi-Hun's line of sight.
Gi-Hun and Ali have the same height.

Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK, denoting the total number of players between Gi-Hun and Ali and the height of both of them respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers, denoting the heights of the players between Gi-Hun and Ali.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the minimum number of players who need to get shot so that Ali is visible in Gi-Hun's line of sight.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and K — the number of players standing between Gi‑Hun and Ali, and the common height of Gi‑Hun and Ali. The second line contains N space‑separated integers H1, H2, ..., HN, the heights of the players in order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the minimum number of players that must be shot so that Ali becomes visible to Gi‑Hun.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: heights greater than 10 are 13 and 16 → 2 players.\nTest case 2: only height 9 is greater than 8 → 1 player.\nTest case 3: all heights are ≤ 6 → 0 players.
Heights greater than 5 are 6 and 7, so 2 players must be shot.
All players are shorter than the line of sight height 100, so no one needs to be shot.

solution.approach: Since Gi‑Hun and Ali have the same height K, the line of sight between them is a horizontal line at height K. Any player whose height is strictly greater than K blocks this line. Players with height ≤ K (including equal) do not block. Therefore, the minimum number of players that must be removed is exactly the count of players with height > K. For each test case we simply iterate over the N heights and increment a counter whenever Hi > K.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, i.e., O(total number of heights) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        k = int(next(it))
        cnt = 0
        for _ in range(n):
            h = int(next(it))
            if h > k:
                cnt += 1
        out_lines.append(str(cnt))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
